notes: Foundational EAF formula (60 GH / 20 KH). Low-buffer, high-SO4 preserves brightness; Mg-only Epsom hardness. Spans light-medium per the 'forgiving' framing in the EAF guide.
EAF Melbourne café-style (St. Ali aesthetic). Slightly more buffer than Holy Water but still Mg-forward; balanced bright cup, light-to-medium specialty range.
Hendon-style high-SO4 (95 ppm) Epsom-heavy water. Brightness-forward; suits light-to-medium specialty roasts where extraction needs lift.
BH 'World in Two Bottles' #4 — moderate buffer (40 KH) with rounded Mg/Na. Reads as the balanced/sweet end of the BH set; spans light-medium.
TWW Espresso profile equivalent. Very high buffer (67.5 KH) and high mineral load (Mg 38.9, K 53, SO4 154, HCO3 82) suit medium-to-dark espresso roasts.
Buffer-only KHCO3 scale-prevention water. Versatile across roast levels; primary value is no-scale operation, secondary is clarity from no-hardness, no-sodium.
EAF formula explicitly designed as 'high buffer water for light roast espresso' per espressoaf.com. Very low Mg (4.9 ppm) + high alkalinity (90 KH) smooths light-roast espresso.

Method flipped to espresso (matches 29th Wave's 60/90 framework; the original 002 migration's filter assignment was likely an oversight, same as 29th was for 004). 3x the Mg of 29th adds body for slightly fuller espresso; spans light-medium.
Hendon's espresso target (24 ppm Mg / 31 KH). Cleaner Mg-only profile at espresso-appropriate concentrations. Medium-roast espresso intent.
Aviary roastery is exclusively very light Nordic-style roasts (Cleveland). Recipe (58 GH / 27 KH, 1.3:1 Mg:Ca) explicitly targets 'cup clarity and articulation' per Feran. Single roast = light.

Aviary's espresso profile — very low hardness (5.5 ppm total Ca+Mg) with high buffer (59.6 KH) and high Na (27 ppm). Round, sweet, smooths light-roast espresso. Old 'Round' tag mapped to Full Body per new taxonomy.
Mg-only Epsom-heavy starter ('based on Holy Water' per migration). Low buffer (15 KH) preserves acidity → light-roast bright.
Migration explicitly notes 'for lighter washed coffees.' Ca-only hardness via CaCl₂ at low buffer. Old 'Delicate' tag mapped to Clarity per new taxonomy.
Mg+Ca blend at 60 GH / 15 KH — the educational pivot toward balanced cups. Spans light/medium specialty range.
Ca-leaning variant (12 Ca / 5 Mg) of Day 3 → adds body and sweetness. Higher Ca pushes toward medium support; still works at light.
Introduces KHCO₃ buffer (sodium-free). Sodium-free, clarity-forward Mg+Ca blend; light-roast oriented.
Complex multi-mineral blend with 15 ppm NaCl boost. More body than earlier days from added salt and the broader mineral palette; spans light/medium.
Low-TDS profile + 20 ppm NaCl — salt-enhanced light brewing. Old 'Delicate' tag merged into Clarity; added Juicy for the salt-enhanced perceived sweetness/fruit.
Sey is the canonical ultra-light Nordic-style washed roaster (Brooklyn). High SO4 (Epsom) + high Cl (CaCl₂) + balanced 15 KH KHCO₃ buffer → vibrant, juicy clarity built for very light washed coffees.

SCA brewing-water target (Ca 51 / Mg 17 / Alk 40). Generic balanced baseline; designed to work across roast levels rather than emphasize any cup character. Single Balanced tag matches its design intent as the universal default. Retagged method=all in migration 009 to surface in both filter and espresso views.

Rao's water target (Ca 20.9 / Mg 8.5 / Alk 40). Lotus-style ratio favoring sweetness and structure over brightness amplification; Rao uses it across both pour-over and espresso. Retagged method=all in migration 009.

Lotus filter recipe (Ca 22.8 / Mg 0 / Alk 24 via KHCO₃ / Cl 40). Mg-free, Ca/Cl-heavy with low buffer — sodium-free, clarity-forward. Classed as classic to match Third Wave Water precedent (commercial brand recipes in classic tray).
Lotus filter recipe (Ca 22.8 / Mg 7.9 / Alk 40 / Na 11.2 / Cl 63.4). Adds Mg + Na for rounded sweetness; higher buffer makes it more roast-flexible than Light and Bright.
Lotus espresso variant (Ca 0 / Mg 3.9 / Alk 44 via KHCO₃ / K 34.7 / Cl 11.5). Clarity-forward espresso: no Ca, sodium-free, K-buffered. Light-roast espresso intent.
Lotus espresso variant (Ca 0 / Mg 3.9 / Alk 56.7 / Na 26.1 / Cl 11.5). High buffer + sodium → 'sweeter, rounder shots' per Lotus description. Old 'Round' tag mapped to Full Body.
Lotus filter recipe (Ca 13 / Mg 7.9 / Alk 16.2 / Cl 46.1). Low buffer for vivid acidity, fruit-forward cups, high clarity per description. The tag pairing (Bright;Juicy) matches the recipe name.

In-house light-roast filter (Ca 2 / Mg 11 / Alk 11 / K 9 / Cl 36 / no SO4). Direct dosing per L: 0.007g CaCl₂·2H₂O + 0.092g MgCl₂·6H₂O + 0.023g KHCO₃ (Lotus-compatible: matches Lotus Calcium and Lotus Magnesium drop chemistry). Distinctive in catalog: only low-TDS filter with zero sulfate. Mg-dominant + Cl-heavy + KHCO₃ buffer = juicy/sweet/clean instead of bright-amplified. Currently in featured tray; natural tray is original.

In-house espresso companion to Cafelytic Filter (Ca 4 / Mg 16 / Alk 32 via KHCO₃ / K 25 / Cl 54 / no SO4). Direct dosing per L: 0.015g CaCl₂·2H₂O + 0.134g MgCl₂·6H₂O + 0.064g KHCO₃ (Lotus-compatible salts). Preserves Cafelytic house character (Cl-heavy, no SO4, sodium-free, K-buffered) at espresso concentrations; Alk 32 sits at the bottom of typical light-roast espresso range. Spans light-medium thanks to espresso buffer expansion.
